metadata.description: Complete scoring function definitions for 115 PRISM risk events. Each event has 3 sub-probabilities (P_preconditions, P_trigger, P_implementation), each with 3-4 observable indicators, normalization functions, and weights.

normalization_types: discrete_map: lookup table mapping specific values to 0-1

dynamic_scoring_functions.scoring_functions.p_preconditions: Dynamic scoring function to compute P(preconditions) from indicators
dynamic_scoring_functions.scoring_functions.p_trigger: Dynamic scoring function to compute P(trigger) from indicators
dynamic_scoring_functions.scoring_functions.p_implementation: Dynamic scoring function to compute P(implementation) from indicators

field_mapping.note: The developer should use scoring_functions for dynamic recalculation. The static p_pre/p_trig/p_impl values serve as calibration baselines.

p_preconditions.justification: FRAGILITY assessment: Top 3 cloud providers (AWS 30%, Azure 20%, GCP 13%) control 63% of cloud infrastructure market (Synergy Research Q2 2025; range 63-70% depending on methodology). Flexera 2025: 70% of organizations use hybrid cloud, avg 2.4 providers. But vendor lock-in is severe: Broadcom VMware acquisition triggered 150-1,500% price increases (CISPE/ECCO 2025 report to European Commission; AT&T faced 1,050% increase). 72-core minimum license effective Apr 2025. Migration complexity creates structural lock-in even as multi-cloud strategies grow.
sources: Flexera State of the Cloud Report 2025 (759 respondents, 70% hybrid)
IDC VMware Cost Increases analysis Dec 2025

p_trigger.justification: CONDITIONAL on P(pre)=0.68: Lock-in problematic during pricing/regulatory shifts. Broadcom-VMware price hikes 300-1200% for some customers (2024). Flexera 2025: 82% of enterprises report cloud cost challenges. Gartner: 70% of cloud spending is with top 3 providers. However, multi-year EAs buffer trigger. Conditional uplift: baseline 0.4 × 1.252 = 0.5008. Concentration score: 0.72/1.0 — AWS+Azure+GCP HHI≈3200 (Synergy 2025). Switching cost: 16.8 months avg migration (Flexera 2025). Bro.
sources: Flexera State of the Cloud Report 2025 (82% cost challenges)

p_implementation.justification: Cloud migration takes 12-24 months (Flexera 2025), but P(impl) measures onset of material impact, not completion. Lock-in impact begins immediately when pricing shock hits (costs rise at renewal). Migration barriers delay escape but the disruption (cost increase, budget impact) materializes within weeks. Pro-rated for 12-month window: immediate cost impact probability high, but migration completion probability low. Net assessment: 0.47 reflects cost impact beginning within 12 months, not migration completing.
sources: Flexera State of the Cloud Report 2025

p_preconditions.justification: Cloud outages +18% in 2024 (Parametrix), 100+ outages across big 3 providers Aug 2024-Aug 2025. However, multi-region and multi-cloud architectures provide resilience for ~35% of enterprises (Gartner 2024). Fragility = systemic dependency × (1 - resilience adoption) = 0.85 × (1 - 0.35 × 0.3) ≈ 0.77. Separates outage frequency from actual vulnerability.

p_trigger.justification: In 2024, 6 outages >10hrs totaling ~100hrs. AWS Oct 2025 lasted ~15hrs. Azure had 50-hour outage. Multiple >4hr events per year.
sources: DemandSage Internet Outage Statistics report Jan 2026

p_implementation.justification: Not all >4hr outages are multi-region. CrowdStrike Jul 2024 affected 8.5M devices globally (multi-region). AWS us-east-1 Dec 2021 was 7hrs but single-region. Uptime Institute: 60% of outages cost >$100K. However, CDN/edge caching limits blast radius.
sources: Uptime Institute Annual Outage Analysis 2024 (60% cost >$100K)

p_preconditions.justification: Cloud pricing generally declining per-unit (AWS has cut prices 100+ times since 2006) but AI/GPU costs rising (NVIDIA H100 cloud instances $2-4/hr). Broadcom VMware licensing changes caused 300-1200% increases for some. GPU pricing volatile: spot H100 prices ranged $1.50-$4.00/hr in 2024.
sources: NVIDIA H100 cloud instance pricing data 2024 ($2-4/hr)

p_trigger.justification: CONDITIONAL on P(pre)=0.57: >30% YoY cost increase extreme for hyperscalers (AWS/Azure/GCP have historically decreased per-unit pricing). Broadcom-style vendor repricing events rare (~1-2/year at enterprise scale). Gartner: cloud spending grew 20% in 2024 to $675B but much is volume-driven. ~30% probability of >30% effective cost increase trigger. Conditional uplift: baseline 0.3 × 1.203 = 0.3609. Concentration score: 0.58/1.0 — Top-3 cloud ~70% share (HHI≈2200). But multi-cloud growing: Flexera 2025 reports 86% pursuing multi-.

p_implementation.justification: Enterprise cloud agreements typically lock rates for 1-3 years, buffering against spot price increases. Gartner reports only ~30% of enterprises on fully consumption-based pricing. Even Broadcom's VMware price hikes took 12-18 months to fully propagate through renewals. ~45% of triggered pricing changes result in >30% effective cost increase within 12 months.
sources: Gartner enterprise cloud agreement pricing analysis 2024-2025

DIG-CLS-003.event_name: Cloud pricing shock / Cost escalation (>30% YoY)

p_preconditions.justification: EU Data Act (2023/2854) effective Sep 2025, GDPR localization requirements, China PIPL (effective Nov 2021, 1.4B population), India DPDP Act 2023 (800M+ internet users) all impose data residency requirements. UNCTAD: 137 countries have data protection laws. Growing number of jurisdictions with conflicting requirements.
sources: China Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL) effective Nov 2021
India Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP) 2023
GDPR data localization enforcement precedents (EDPB guidance 2024)

p_trigger.justification: Data sovereignty enforcement increasing. EU-US Data Privacy Framework (3rd attempt, adopted Jul 2023) faces legal challenges from noyb. EDPB issued 1,901 GDPR fines totaling €4.5B through 2024. China PIPL enforcement: CAC fined Didi $1.2B Jul 2022.
sources: EDPB GDPR enforcement statistics 2024 (1,901 fines, €4.5B total)
China CAC Didi PIPL fine Jul 2022 ($1.2B)

p_implementation.justification: GDPR enforcement actions take 6-18 months from complaint to decision (EDPB data). 1,901 GDPR fines totaling €4.5B issued through 2024, but most relate to processing violations not data residency. Cross-border enforcement coordination under EDPB adds delays.

p_preconditions.justification: Postman 2024: 89% of developers use third-party APIs (exposure). However, exposure ≠ fragility: ~40% of enterprises maintain abstraction layers or multi-vendor API strategies (Gartner 2024). Broadcom VMware licensing changes (2024) showed real disruption potential for deeply embedded APIs. Fragility assessment: 0.89 exposure × 0.73 single-vendor dependency = 0.65 after accounting for mitigation.

p_trigger.justification: CONDITIONAL on P(pre)=0.63: Major API/platform deprecations occur ~5-8 per year at enterprise scale. Postman State of API 2025: 82% of organizations are API-first, making deprecation impactful. Google deprecated 20+ APIs in 2023; Broadcom VMware licensing changes (2024) affected thousands of enterprises. Lunar.dev 2024 survey: 36% of companies spend more time troubleshooting APIs than developing features. Twitter/X API pricing change (2023) eliminated 71% of third-party app ecosystem (Similarweb data). ~42% annual probability of a deprecation materially affecting a specific enterprise software stack. Conditional uplift: baseline 0.42 × 1.147 = 0.4817. Concentration score: 0.42/1.0 — API dependency broad but alternatives usually exist. Postman 2025: 82% API-first but microservice ar.
sources: Postman State of the API Report 2025 (82% API-first, 5,700 respondents)
Lunar.dev API Consumer Survey 2024 (36% troubleshooting > development)

p_implementation.justification: API deprecations typically provide 6-18 month migration windows (OpenAI averages 3-6 months, Google provides 12 months). Postman 2025: 17% of organizations use no API monitoring tools. However, large enterprises can often adapt within the window. Broadcom VMware changes propagated through renewals over 12-18 months. ~55% probability of material impact (missed SLAs, forced migration costs) beginning within 12 months of deprecation announcement.
sources: OpenAI API Deprecation timeline (3-6 month windows)
Postman State of the API 2025 (17% no monitoring tools)

p_preconditions.justification: AI demand driving massive data center buildout. CBRE: North Virginia vacancy <2%, Frankfurt <5%. Power queue 3-5 years in Amsterdam, Singapore moratorium. IEA: data center electricity demand to double by 2026 (reaching 1,000 TWh). McKinsey: $2T+ data center investment needed by 2030.
sources: CBRE Data Center Global Report H2 2024 (vacancy rates)
IEA Electricity 2024 report (data center demand doubling to 1,000 TWh by 2026)

p_trigger.justification: CONDITIONAL on P(pre)=0.67: Shortages emerging in specific markets. Singapore moratorium 2019-2022. Dublin power constraints. Conditional uplift: baseline 0.4 × 1.1575 = 0.463. Concentration score: 0.45/1.0 — Data center vacancy <5% (CBRE 2024) but geographic concentration varies. Power constraints: N.Virgin.

p_implementation.justification: Data center capacity shortages manifest as extended wait times (6-18 months for new builds) and rising prices rather than hard shutdowns. CBRE reports Northern Virginia vacancy <2% and Amsterdam power queue 3-5 years. However, cloud providers pre-reserve capacity.
sources: CBRE Data Center Global Report H2 2024
Uptime Institute Global Data Center Survey 2024

DIG-CLS-006.event_name: National cloud / Data center capacity shortage

p_preconditions.justification: FRAGILITY assessment: TSMC dominance varies by node — 90%+ at 3nm (TrendForce 2024), 70-80% at 5nm, 64-66% overall foundry market (IDC 2024: 64%, projected 66% in 2025). Advanced nodes (<7nm) = 69-73% of TSMC revenue (Q2 2025 earnings). Samsung foundry share fell to 7.2% in Q2 2025. At cutting edge (<5nm), TSMC approaches near-monopoly. ASML EUV monopoly (100%) creates additional chokepoint. US BIS export controls (Oct 2022, Oct 2023, Dec 2024) actively exploit this concentration. Zero short-term alternatives for advanced AI/HPC chips.
sources: TSMC Q2 2025 earnings (73% revenue from <7nm nodes)
SemiWiki advanced silicon analysis Jul 2025 (TSMC 64% advanced logic)

p_trigger.justification: CONDITIONAL on P(pre)=0.79: Controls expanded annually since 2022. Section 232 tariffs under consideration. Conditional uplift: baseline 0.6 × 1.322 = 0.7932. Concentration score: 0.92/1.0 — TSMC 92% advanced node share = near-monopoly HHI≈8500. Zero short-term alternatives for <5nm. Export.

p_implementation.justification: Controls effective in 30-90 days. But chip supply chains have 6-12 month lead times.

p_preconditions.justification: TSMC produces ~60% of global foundry output and ~90% of advanced (<7nm) chips (SIA 2024). Taiwan, Japan, South Korea in Pacific Ring of Fire seismic zone. Taiwan experienced 7.2 magnitude earthquake Apr 2024 (TSMC evacuated fabs, production resumed in 1-3 days). Japan 2011 Tohoku earthquake disrupted semiconductor supply for months.
sources: SIA Semiconductor Industry Factbook 2024 (TSMC market share)
Taiwan Apr 2024 earthquake TSMC impact assessment

p_trigger.justification: Major fab disruptions: Renesas fire 2021, Texas storm 2021. ~3-4 per decade. Annual ~0.35.

p_implementation.justification: Fab disruptions cause 3-6 month shortages. Renesas took 3 months to restore.

DIG-HWS-002.event_name: Semiconductor supply chain disruption (fab fire, earthquake)

p_preconditions.justification: Mature node (>28nm) investment slowed. Auto MCU supply recovering from 2021-2023. China expanding capacity. Moderate.
sources: SIA Semiconductor Mature Node Capacity Report 2024

p_trigger.justification: Major legacy shortage 2021-2023. Some long lead times persist.

p_implementation.justification: Legacy shortages persist 6-18 months. 2021-2023 lasted ~2 years. ~60% material impact.

DIG-HWS-003.event_name: Legacy chip / Mature node shortage (automotive MCUs)

p_preconditions.justification: Hardware supply chain compromise concerns elevated after SuperMicro allegations (2018, later disputed), and Lebanon pager attacks (Sep 2024) demonstrated feasibility. US CHIPS Act Section 9902 includes supply chain security provisions. CISA ICT Supply Chain Risk Management guidance updated 2024. Moderate but not dominant risk.
sources: US CHIPS and Science Act Section 9902 supply chain provisions
CISA ICT Supply Chain Risk Management guidance 2024

p_trigger.justification: Confirmed major compromises rare (~1-2/decade). Lebanon pager attack 2024 showed feasibility.

p_implementation.justification: Hardware compromises extremely hard to detect. Bloomberg SuperMicro investigation (2018) showed multi-year detection lag. Lebanon pager attack Sep 2024 demonstrated explosive hardware tampering feasibility. Remediation requires hardware replacement (months/years). Average breach detection time 204 days (IBM Cost of Data Breach 2024).
sources: IBM Cost of a Data Breach Report 2024 (204 days avg detection)

DIG-HWS-004.event_name: Hardware supply chain attack / Compromised components

p_preconditions.justification: ASML is the sole EUV lithography supplier globally ($27.6B revenue 2024). EUV systems cost ~$380M each with 12-24 month lead times. ASML backlog was €36B at end 2024. US/Japan/Netherlands export controls restrict EUV sales to China since Jan 2024.

p_trigger.justification: CONDITIONAL on P(pre)=0.61: Shortages driven by buildout cycle and export controls. ASML delivered 380 lithography systems in 2024 (vs 400+ demand). EUV lead times 18+ months. CHIPS Act subsidies driving simultaneous fab builds (TSMC Arizona, Samsung Taylor TX, Intel Ohio). Conditional uplift: baseline 0.35 × 1.287 = 0.4504. Concentration score: 0.82/1.0 — ASML 100% EUV monopoly (HHI=10000). Applied/Lam/TEL oligopoly in deposition/etch (HHI≈3500). €36B ba.
sources: CHIPS Act fab construction timeline data

p_implementation.justification: Equipment shortages slow-building. Lead times 18-24 months.

p_preconditions.justification: Siemens (25%), Rockwell (12%), ABB (10%), Schneider (9%) control ~56% of global industrial automation market (ARC Advisory 2024). Proprietary protocols (PROFINET, EtherNet/IP, Modbus) create deep vendor lock-in. OT/IT convergence increasing dependency. ~75% of factories use single-vendor PLC architecture (Gartner OT survey). Moderate-high preconditions.

p_trigger.justification: ICS/SCADA lock-in problematic at EOL, licensing changes, or sanctions. Siemens Russia exit Mar 2022 affected 120+ factories dependent on Siemens PLCs. Rockwell Automation lifecycle: typical PLC support 15-20 years. Honewell announced price increases of 8-12% across industrial automation 2024.
sources: Siemens Russia business exit Mar 2022 (120+ factories affected)

p_implementation.justification: Industrial automation replacement cycles are 15-30 years (ARC Advisory Group). Migration from one PLC/DCS vendor to another takes 2-5 years minimum. However, parts/firmware support withdrawal has immediate impact. Siemens Russia exit left factories without spare parts within months. ~47% of trigger events cause material impact within 12 months.
sources: ARC Advisory Group industrial automation lifecycle analysis 2024

DIG-HWS-006.event_name: Industrial automation hardware dependency / Vendor lock-in

p_preconditions.justification: Japan and Germany produce ~55% of global machine tool output (Gardner World Machine Tool Output Survey 2024). Japan exported $9.2B in machine tools in 2023. China remains dependent on imports for high-precision 5-axis CNC machines. Export control expansion via Wassenaar Arrangement targets dual-use equipment. Moderate preconditions.
sources: Gardner World Machine Tool Output Survey 2024
Japan Machine Tool Builders Association export data 2023

p_trigger.justification: Machine tool controls expanding with semiconductor controls. Japan restricted exports to China 2023-2024.

p_implementation.justification: Export restrictions take effect within 60-90 days of announcement. US BIS Entity List additions effective immediately for listed items. Japan machine tool export controls (Jul 2023) affected $9.2B export market. Existing installed base continues operating but spare parts and upgrades restricted.
sources: US BIS Entity List effective dates
Japan machine tool export value data 2023 ($9.2B JMTBA)

DIG-HWS-007.event_name: Machine tool / Manufacturing equipment access restriction

p_preconditions.justification: SAP, Oracle, Microsoft, Autodesk dominate. SAP forcing S/4HANA migration by 2027. Russia lost Western licenses 2022. Geopolitical risks.

p_trigger.justification: CONDITIONAL on P(pre)=0.57: Mass enterprise software terminations rare—primarily sanctions-driven. SAP gave Russian clients until 2024 to migrate (affects 1,400+ companies). Oracle, Microsoft similarly exited Russia. Individual licensing disputes more common (~50/year). Adobe terminated Venezuelan accounts 2019. Conditional uplift: baseline 0.25 × 1.224 = 0.306. Concentration score: 0.64/1.0 — SAP+Oracle 60%+ ERP share (HHI≈2500). Migration: 17.4 months avg (Panorama 2024). Sanctions-triggere.
sources: SAP Russia exit timeline 2022-2024 (1,400+ companies affected)

p_implementation.justification: Sanctions-driven termination is immediate and absolute. SAP Russia deadlines were firm with no extensions. ERP migration typically takes 12-24 months (Panorama Consulting 2024 ERP survey: average implementation 17.4 months). ~65% materialize within 12 months given contractual notice periods.
sources: Panorama Consulting Group 2024 ERP Report (17.4 months avg implementation)

DIG-SWS-001.event_name: Critical software license termination (ERP, CAD, OS)

p_preconditions.justification: 96%+ codebases contain OSS (Synopsys OSSRA 2024). Critical packages maintained by single devs. xz utils backdoor 2024. High dependency.

p_trigger.justification: CONDITIONAL on P(pre)=0.73: Major open-source supply chain events: Log4j (Dec 2021), Spring4Shell (2022), PyTorch compromise (2022), xz utils backdoor (Mar 2024). Sonatype documented ~245,000 malicious packages in 2024 (up 156% YoY). ~1-2 events of enterprise-significant severity per year. Conditional uplift: baseline 0.52 × 1.1715 = 0.6092. Concentration score: 0.49/1.0 — OpenSSF Census III, 2024: 25% critical projects have ≤2 maintainers. But core infrastructure (Linux .
sources: Sonatype State of the Software Supply Chain Report 2024
CISA advisories for Log4j (AA21-356A) and xz utils (2024)

p_implementation.justification: Severity varies enormously. Log4j (Dec 2021) affected ~70% of Java applications globally (Sonatype). MOVEit breach (May 2023) compromised 2,700+ organizations. xz utils (Mar 2024) caught before widespread exploitation. Many smaller supply chain events affect <100 organizations.
sources: Sonatype Log4j impact analysis (70% of Java apps affected)

DIG-SWS-002.event_name: Open-source maintainer risk / Supply chain failure (Log4j)

p_preconditions.justification: OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, and Meta account for ~80% of frontier model API usage (Stanford HAI 2024). Enterprise AI adoption: 72% of organizations now use AI in at least one function (McKinsey Global Survey 2024). EU AI Act classifies high-risk systems with compliance requirements. Growing but concentrated dependency.

p_trigger.justification: CONDITIONAL on P(pre)=0.67: AI access could be restricted by EU AI Act (effective Aug 2024, full compliance by Aug 2026), US executive orders, or vendor decision. OpenAI blocked access from China, Russia, Iran (Jun 2024). EU AI Act bans certain systems; classifies others as high-risk. Market young and volatile. Conditional uplift: baseline 0.35 × 1.133 = 0.3965. Concentration score: 0.38/1.0 — AI platform market less concentrated than cloud: OpenAI/Google/Anthropic/Meta compete. But model-spe.

p_implementation.justification: AI model restrictions would propagate through API changes, license modifications, or regulatory enforcement. However, open-source alternatives (Llama, Mistral, DeepSeek) and multi-provider strategies reduce single-vendor dependency. Stanford HAI AI Index 2024 shows 149 foundation models released in 2023 (65% open-source).
sources: Hugging Face Open LLM Leaderboard model availability data

DIG-SWS-003.event_name: AI/ML platform dependency / Model access restriction

p_preconditions.justification: MITRE ATLAS catalogs 30+ adversarial ML attack techniques. NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0) identifies data poisoning and adversarial inputs as key risks. Enterprise AI deployment: 72% of organizations using AI (McKinsey 2024). Only 38% have AI risk mitigation processes (IBM AI Adoption Index 2024). Moderate preconditions as field matures.
sources: MITRE ATLAS (Adversarial Threat Landscape for AI Systems) framework v4.0 2024
NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0) Jan 2023
IBM AI Adoption Index 2024 (risk mitigation statistics)

p_trigger.justification: Confirmed production attacks rare (~1-2 documented). Research demos common.

p_implementation.justification: AI adversarial effects can be subtle. MITRE ATLAS catalogs 30+ attack techniques. Microsoft Tay chatbot manipulation (2016) was immediate and visible. Training data poisoning may go undetected for months. Gartner: only 25% of organizations have AI testing/monitoring frameworks (2024).
sources: MITRE ATLAS framework v4.0 (30+ attack techniques)
Gartner AI governance survey 2024 (25% have monitoring frameworks)

p_preconditions.justification: FRAGILITY assessment (not mere exposure): Supply chain attacks +742% since 2019 (Sonatype). SolarWinds (2020), Log4j (2021), MOVEit (2023), xz (2024). High structural risk.

p_trigger.justification: CONDITIONAL on P(pre)=0.82: Critical events: SolarWinds, Kaseya, Log4j, MOVEit, xz. ~1-2/year. Conditional uplift: baseline 0.65 × 1.182 = 0.7683. Concentration score: 0.52/1.0 — Synopsys 2024: 84% codebases with known vulnerabilities. Sonatype: 245K malicious packages (+156%). .

p_implementation.justification: Not all zero-days widely exploited before patching. MOVEit (CVE-2023-34362) exploited by Cl0p, affected 2,700+ orgs. Log4Shell patched within 2 weeks but exploitation began within 24 hours. Mandiant: average zero-day exploitation window was 5 days in 2023 before patch.

p_preconditions.justification: 5G restrictions on Huawei/ZTE largely implemented in US (Secure Equipment Act 2021), UK (2027 ban), and EU (23 member states with restrictions). Quantum computing export controls included in US outbound investment rules (Jan 2025). Technologies still maturing—quantum not yet commercially deployed at scale. Moderate preconditions.
sources: US Treasury outbound investment rules quantum provisions Jan 2025
European 5G Toolbox member state implementation reports

p_trigger.justification: 5G restrictions largely implemented (US Secure Equipment Act 2021, UK 2027 ban, 23 EU member states). Quantum computing nascent—no commercial deployment at scale. US Treasury outbound investment rules (Jan 2025) target quantum. Only 3-5 new technology restriction categories/year.
sources: US Treasury outbound investment final rule Jan 2025 (quantum restrictions)

p_implementation.justification: Restrictions take 12-24 months typically. 5G rip-and-replace took 2+ years.

p_preconditions.justification: Autodesk ($5.1B revenue), Siemens Digital Industries Software ($6B), Dassault Systèmes ($6.4B), PTC ($2.1B) dominate CAD/CAM/PLM with ~70% market share (CIMdata 2024). Proprietary file formats (DWG, NX, CATIA) create deep lock-in. Autodesk eliminated perpetual licenses in 2023, forcing subscription migration. High dependency.

p_trigger.justification: CONDITIONAL on P(pre)=0.67: Events: Autodesk eliminated perpetual licenses 2023 (forcing 5.5M subscribers to subscription). Broadcom VMware licensing caused 300-1200% increases. Sanctions-driven software loss (SAP, Oracle in Russia). Relatively infrequent at enterprise-disrupting severity. Conditional uplift: baseline 0.25 × 1.168 = 0.292. Concentration score: 0.48/1.0 — Siemens/Dassault/PTC ~80% industrial CAD/PLM (HHI≈2800). Proprietary file formats, but open standard.

p_implementation.justification: CAD/PLM migration takes 2-5 years for full platform change. However, P(impl) measures onset of material disruption, not migration completion. Licensing changes (Autodesk perpetual → subscription) cause immediate cost impact. Sanctions-driven loss (Russia) causes immediate access loss. Within 12-month window: licensing cost impact is immediate (onset in weeks), capability loss requires workarounds. Pro-rated: 0.55 reflects probability disruption begins within 12 months.

p_preconditions.justification: Siemens (25%), Rockwell (12%), ABB (10%), Schneider (9%) control ~56% of global DCS/PLC market (ARC Advisory 2024). Proprietary protocols (PROFINET, EtherNet/IP, Modbus) create deep vendor lock-in. Typical ICS lifecycle 15-30 years.
sources: ARC Advisory Group DCS/PLC market share data 2024 (top 4 = 56%)

p_trigger.justification: CONDITIONAL on P(pre)=0.62: MES/SCADA vendor disruptions infrequent but impactful. Sanctions on Siemens Russia affected factories. Dragos: ~600 ICS advisories 2024. Conditional uplift: baseline 0.25 × 1.238 = 0.3095. Concentration score: 0.68/1.0 — Siemens/Rockwell/ABB oligopoly in DCS/SCADA (HHI≈2600). OPC-UA adoption <40%. Physical rewiring requ.

p_implementation.justification: ICS/SCADA disruptions have immediate operational impact. Colonial Pipeline ransomware (May 2021) shut 5,500-mile pipeline for 6 days. Oldsmar FL water treatment hack (Feb 2021) attempted to raise NaOH to dangerous levels. ICS migration costs typically $5M-$50M (Gartner).
sources: Colonial Pipeline ransomware May 2021 (5,500-mile shutdown, 6 days)

p_preconditions.justification: Air freight demand reached 68.7 million tonnes in 2024 (IATA), a 12.4% increase. Boeing delivered only 348 aircraft in 2024 (vs. 806 in 2018). E-commerce cross-border air freight grew 30%+ (Freightos). Red Sea disruption diverted some ocean freight to air. Moderate-high capacity pressure.
sources: IATA Air Freight Market Analysis December 2024

p_trigger.justification: Shortages: COVID 2020-2021, periodic peak crunches.

p_implementation.justification: Seasonal and regional. IATA: air cargo demand +11.3% in 2024 (record). TAC Index: Hong Kong-North America rates peaked at $5.50/kg in Q4 2024 (vs $3.50 in Q1). Red Sea diversion shifted ocean-to-air, boosting spot rates 20-40%. ~55% of capacity events cause material rate spikes (>25% increase) within 12 months.
sources: IATA Air Freight Market Analysis 2024 (11.3% demand growth)
TAC Index Air Freight Rate Benchmark 2024 ($5.50/kg peak HKG-NAM)

p_preconditions.justification: CrowdStrike Jul 2024 outage grounded 5,000+ flights globally. Gatwick drone incident Dec 2018 closed airport for 36 hours (1,000 flights cancelled). Weather closures: FAA reports ~45,000 flight delays/day during severe weather events. Istanbul airport fog caused 3-day cargo backlog Dec 2023.
sources: CrowdStrike Jul 2024 aviation impact (5,000+ flights grounded)
Gatwick drone incident Dec 2018 (36 hours closure)

p_trigger.justification: Closures >24hrs: ~3-5/year globally. CrowdStrike multi-day.
sources: Eurocontrol Network Operations Report 2024 (airport closure data)
FAA OPSNET Air Traffic Operations data 2024

p_implementation.justification: Most >24hr airport closures significantly impact air freight. CrowdStrike outage caused 3-5 day cargo backlogs. Gatwick drone: £50M estimated losses. Weather events: Chicago O'Hare (major cargo hub) averaged 3 closures >24hr annually. ~60% of >24hr closures materially disrupt cargo operations.
sources: Eurocontrol Network Operations Annual Report 2024 (airport closure impact data)
FAA OPSNET delay and cancellation statistics 2024

p_preconditions.justification: FedEx ($87B revenue), UPS ($91B), DHL ($22B cargo) dominate air cargo. Smaller carriers (Atlas Air acquired at $5.2B in 2023, Kalitta Air, Polar Air Cargo) face margin pressure. IATA: airline industry net profit $31B in 2024 (healthy overall). Low-moderate preconditions for major carrier failure.

p_trigger.justification: Very rare for major carriers. FedEx ($87B revenue), UPS ($91B), DHL ($22B cargo) are financially stable. Smaller cargo airlines: Atlas Air acquired $5.2B (2023), Kalitta Air private. Allianz Trade: 474 large companies went bankrupt globally in 2024. Airline bankruptcies rare in cargo sector.
sources: Allianz Trade Global Insolvency Report 2025 (474 large bankruptcies globally)

p_implementation.justification: Bankruptcy immediately removes capacity. Hanjin Shipping analogy: cargo stranded globally. Atlas Air (acquired 2023 for $5.2B) operated 100+ aircraft—failure would remove significant trans-Pacific capacity. Cargo airline bankruptcy rarer than passenger but impact acute on specific routes.
sources: IATA air cargo capacity by carrier data

p_preconditions.justification: Jet fuel averaged $2.72/gallon in 2024 (IATA Jet Fuel Monitor), down from $3.90 peak in 2022. SAF mandates: EU ReFuelEU requires 2% SAF blend from 2025 (rising to 70% by 2050). SAF costs 2-5x conventional jet fuel. US refinery capacity concentrated (5 companies control 55%). Moderate preconditions.

p_trigger.justification: Major shortages rare. Price spikes >50%: 2008, 2022.

p_implementation.justification: Fuel price spikes pass through to freight rates within 2-4 weeks via fuel surcharges. Jet fuel averaged $2.72/gal in 2024 (IATA), down from $3.90 peak 2022. SAF costs 2-5x conventional ($4-8/gal). IATA: fuel is 25-30% of airline operating costs. ~55% of fuel price spikes (>30%) materially impact air freight costs.
sources: IATA airline cost structure data (fuel = 25-30% of costs)

OPS-AIR-004.event_name: Aviation Fuel Shortage or Price Spike

p_preconditions.justification: Russian airspace closed to 36 countries since Feb 2022 (adding 2-4 hours to EU-Asia routes). Middle East: Eurocontrol reports periodic flight restrictions over Syria, Iraq, Iran. Iceland's Eyjafjallajökull 2010 closed European airspace for 6 days ($1.7B airline losses).
sources: Eurocontrol Russian airspace closure impact 2022-2024 (36 countries affected)

p_trigger.justification: Russia closure ongoing (since Feb 2022). Middle East restrictions periodic (Iran-Israel tensions Oct 2024 caused 48hr airspace restrictions). Volcanic events: 2-3 significant ash events per decade. Combined probability of new significant restriction.
